dataset: DHS CFATS Appendix A - Chemicals of Interest

pi_items.source: CISA CFATS Appendix A and Federal Register Notice (72 FR 65396)
CISA CFATS documentation: 500 lbs when chemical presents theft hazard; 2,500 lbs when presents release hazard

pi_items.verified_value: Theft/Diversion (for 500 lb STQ) or Release-Toxic (for 2,500 lb STQ)
pi_items.source: CISA CFATS documentation shows 500 lbs is for theft hazard, 2,500 lbs for release (toxic) hazard. 'Toxic' keyword associates with release, not theft.
CISA CFATS and Federal Register Notice (March 21, 2008) on Propane clarification

CISA CFATS documentation, Federal Register: 'The screening threshold quantity for propane is 60,000 lbs, and facilities need not count propane in tanks of 10,000 lbs or less.' The 10,000 lbs is an exclusion rule, not the STQ.
CISA CFATS documentation: Propane is classified as 'Release-Flammable' chemical of interest
CISA CFATS Appendix A and multiple DHS guidance documents
CISA CFATS documentation: 400 pounds for theft/diversion security threat (when packaged for transportation); 5,000 pounds for release-explosive threat
CISA CFATS documentation shows Ammonium Nitrate listed under theft/diversion category
CISA CFATS Appendix A Chemicals of Interest list
CISA CFATS documentation: 220 pounds STQ for Theft (CW/CWP - Chemical Weapons Precursors) with 80% minimum concentration
CISA CFATS documentation lists Triethanolamine under theft/diversion category

Comprehensive web search of CISA, DHS, Federal Register documents on CFATS Appendix A showed no mention of sodium chloride as a regulated chemical of interest. Note: CISA references sodium chlorate (different chemical) as not regulated; sodium chloride (table salt) does not appear in CFATS COI list.
CISA CFATS Appendix A and CISA Hydrogen Peroxide Flyer
CISA CFATS documentation: 400 lbs STQ for hydrogen peroxide in transportation packaging at concentration 35% or higher
CISA CFATS documentation: Hydrogen peroxide listed as theft/diversion COI for EXP/IEDP (Explosives/Improvised Explosive Device Precursors)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves verifying DHS CFATS (Chemical Facility Anti-Terrorism Standards) compliance data for six chemicals. Verified facts: (1) Chlorine IS regulated under CFATS with an STQ of 500 lbs for theft hazard and 2,500 lbs for release hazard; (2) Propane IS regulated with an STQ of 60,000 lbs (NOT 10,000 lbs as claimed - 10,000 lbs is merely an exclusion threshold), classified as Release-Flammable; (3) Ammonium Nitrate IS regulated with an STQ of 400 lbs for theft/diversion; (4) Triethanolamine IS regulated with an STQ of 220 lbs for theft/diversion; (5) Sodium Chloride is NOT regulated under CFATS; (6) Hydrogen Peroxide IS regulated with an STQ of 400 lbs (35% concentration or higher in transportation packaging) for theft/diversion. CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: The metadata lists Propane's STQ as 10,000 lbs when it should be 60,000 lbs. Additionally, Chlorine's security issue keyword 'Toxic' is misaligned - 'Toxic' is the hazard type for the 2,500 lb release threshold, not the 500 lb theft threshold. All other chemicals and their STQs are accurate.
